frameworks.name: theory of mind
frameworks.one_line: attribution of mental states to self and others

frameworks.name: simulation theory of mindreading
frameworks.one_line: self-experience as route to inferring others' beliefs

frameworks.name: false-belief understanding
frameworks.one_line: signature test of representing others' divergent views

core_question: Do great apes draw on their own prior experience to anticipate an agent acting under a false belief?
key_assumptions: non-verbal paradigm is valid across great-ape species
manipulations: prior pretest experience: deceived vs. naive apes
agent's belief state in the test scene (false vs. true)
key_findings: first looks were predicted by apes' own prior experience with the stimulus
previously-deceived apes anticipated the agent's false-belief-guided action
naive apes did not anticipate the false-belief-driven action
interpretations: great-ape false-belief understanding uses self-experience as a simulation route
nonhuman primates can leverage personal experience to track others' mental states
